; CHECK-NEXT:       REPLICATE store ir<2>, vp<[[PTR]]>
; CHECK-NEXT:     Successor(s): pred.store.continue
; CHECK-EMPTY:
; CHECK-NEXT:     pred.store.continue:
; CHECK-NEXT:     No successors
; CHECK-NEXT:   }
; CHECK-NEXT:   Successor(s): default.2
; CHECK-EMPTY:
; CHECK-NEXT:   default.2:
; CHECK-NEXT:     EMIT vp<[[CAN_CMP:%.+]]> = add nuw vp<[[CAN_IV]]>, vp<[[VFxUF]]>
; CHECK-NEXT:     EMIT branch-on-count vp<[[CAN_CMP]]>, vp<[[VTC]]>
; CHECK-NEXT:   No successors
; CHECK-NEXT: }
; CHECK-NEXT: Successor(s): middle.block
; CHECK-EMPTY:
; CHECK-NEXT: middle.block:
; CHECK-NEXT:   EMIT vp<[[MIDDLE_CMP:%.+]]> = icmp eq vp<[[TC]]>, vp<[[VTC]]>
; CHECK-NEXT:   EMIT branch-on-cond vp<[[MIDDLE_CMP]]>
; CHECK-NEXT: Successor(s): ir-bb<exit>, scalar.ph
; CHECK-EMPTY:
; CHECK-NEXT: ir-bb<exit>:
; CHECK-NEXT: No successors
; CHECK-EMPTY:
; CHECK-NEXT: scalar.ph:
; CHECK-NEXT: No successors
; CHECK-NEXT: }
entry:
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %ptr.iv = phi ptr [ %start, %entry ], [ %ptr.iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %l = load i8, ptr %ptr.iv, align 1
  switch i8 %l, label %default [
    i8 -12, label %if.then.1
    i8 13, label %if.then.2
    i8 0, label %default
  ]

if.then.1:                                        ; preds = %loop.header
  store i8 42, ptr %ptr.iv, align 1
  br label %loop.latch

if.then.2:                                        ; preds = %loop.header
  store i8 0, ptr %ptr.iv, align 1
  br label %loop.latch

default:                                          ; preds = %loop.header, %loop.header
  store i8 2, ptr %ptr.iv, align 1
  br label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %default, %if.then.2, %if.then.1
  %ptr.iv.next = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr.iv, i64 1
  %ec = icmp eq ptr %ptr.iv.next, %end
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch
  ret void
}

define dso_local void @test(ptr %start, ptr %end) {
entry:
  br label %header

header:                                           ; preds = %latch, %entry
  %ptr = phi ptr [ %start, %entry ], [ %ptr.next, %latch ]
  %i11 = icmp ne ptr %ptr, %end
  br i1 %i11, label %bb12, label %exit

bb12:                                             ; preds = %header
  %val = load i32, ptr %ptr, align 4
  %c1 = icmp eq i32 %val, 13
  %c2 = icmp eq i32 %val, -12
  %c3 = or i1 %c1, %c2
  br i1 %c3, label %store, label %latch

store:                                            ; preds = %bb12
  store i32 42, ptr %ptr, align 4

  %ptr.next = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %ptr, i32 1
  br label %header

  ret void

issue.title: Missed loop replace_if vectorization due to the presence of switch
issue.author: davidbolvansky

comments.author: davidbolvansky
comments.body:
Or just delay SimplifyBranchOnICmpChain? Something like
https://reviews.llvm.org/D84108 ; to avoid creating switch too early.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: We should at least adjust the heuristic to stop forming switches from two comparisons. I've seen this block optimizations a few times already, and I can't really imagine a case where this would be beneficial for optimization or lowering.

comments.author: rotateright
comments.body:
> So either simplifycfg should not create switches before vectorizers (easy
> fix, maybe profitable for other optimizations too?), and/or vectorizers
> should know how to handle them.

I think the 1st option is more likely to succeed.
It even looks like we already have the opposite transform in SimplifyCFG -- ConvertTwoCaseSwitch() ?

comments.author: hiraditya
comments.body: If vectorizer were to handle this, there should be a way to roll it back if vectorization wasn't successful.

comments.author: hiraditya
comments.body: OTOH we generate jump table in AArch64 backend only when the number of case statements is more than 12 https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/98391
